forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a280
+project: diagnostics
+title: Route ALL follower LSP code-context ops through the leader (not just diagnostics)
+---
+## Why (evidence, 2026-06-19 investigation)
+Follower processes spawn NO LSP supervisor (correct, per ^7a5h2bj). The follower→leader multiplexer (^ref6nj4) + its live wiring (^4rjtgsj) route ONLY the diagnostics tool to the leader. Every OTHER LSP-backed code-context op silently degrades on a follower to tree-sitter / the persisted index, never crossing the socket to the leader's live rust-analyzer.
+
+Evidence:
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/diagnostics/mod.rs::diagnose_via_leader` (`:426`) is the ONLY production caller of `SessionRequestClient`.
+- All other ops (`get_definition`, `get_type_definition`, `get_hover`, `get_references`, `get_implementations`, `workspace_symbol_live`, `get_inbound_calls`, `get_code_actions`, `get_rename_edits`, …) build a `LayeredContext` from the LOCAL `LSP_SUPERVISOR` (`lsp_session_for_file`/`any_lsp_session`, `code_context/mod.rs:57/83`), which a follower never initializes → `session = None`.
+- `LayeredContext::lsp_request` (`layered_context.rs:234`) short-circuits `None → Ok(None)`, so live ops fall back to tree-sitter/persisted layers with no indication they were degraded by the leader/follower split.
+- The multiplexer's generic `lsp_request` method (`request_api.rs::dispatch`) is wired on the LEADER side but has NO production follower-side caller (only tests, `leader_follower_request_ipc.rs:141`).
+
+Net: on a follower (e.g. a stdio-MCP subagent), go-to-definition / hover / references / symbols / implementations / inbound-calls / code-actions / rename return tree-sitter-only or empty results instead of the leader's real rust-analyzer answers.
+
+## What
+Make `LayeredContext` (or the op layer) route its live-LSP requests to the leader over the EXISTING `SessionRequestClient`/`lsp_request` multiplexer when there is no local session, instead of returning `Ok(None)`. Reuse the existing transport — do NOT add a second client/socket/envelope. A follower op should: try local session → if none, route `lsp_request(method, params)` to the leader via `SessionRequestClient` (built from `workspace.socket_path()`/`lock_path()`); on connect failure surface the typed not-leader/leader-pid error (or fall back to the index layer where that is the documented best-effort behavior — decide per op and be explicit).
+
+## Depends on
+- ^ref6nj4 (multiplexer mechanism), ^4rjtgsj (live wiring + SessionRequestClient construction pattern to mirror) — both done.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] On a follower process, the live code-context LSP ops (definition/type-definition/hover/references/implementations/workspace+document symbols/inbound-calls/code-actions/rename) route to the leader's session over the socket and return the leader's real results — not silent tree-sitter/empty degradation.
+- [ ] Reuses the existing `SessionRequestClient`/`lsp_request` path; no second transport/client/envelope.
+- [ ] Connect/serve failure surfaces the typed not-leader/leader-pid error (or documented index fallback), never a silent wrong-empty.
+- [ ] Integration (gated on rust-analyzer): a follower `sah` process running e.g. get_references / get_hover gets the leader's real answer with only the leader's single rust-analyzer running.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`. The `LayeredContext::lsp_request None→Ok(None)` seam is the natural injection point. #diagnostics
\ No newline at end of file

+++ modified

-- `LayeredContext::lsp_request` (`layered_context.rs:234`) short-circuits `None → Ok(None)`, so live ops fall back to tree-sitter/persisted layers with no indication they were degraded by the leader/follower split.

-

-- ^ref6nj4 (multiplexer mechanism), ^4rjtgsj (live wiring + SessionRequestClient construction pattern to mirror) — both done.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] On a follower process, the live code-context LSP ops (definition/type-definition/hover/references/implementations/workspace+document symbols/inbound-calls/code-actions/rename) route to the leader's session over the socket and return the leader's real results — not silent tree-sitter/empty degradation.
-- [ ] Reuses the existing `SessionRequestClient`/`lsp_request` path; no second transport/client/envelope.
-- [ ] Connect/serve failure surfaces the typed not-leader/leader-pid error (or documented index fallback), never a silent wrong-empty.
-- [ ] Integration (gated on rust-analyzer): a follower `sah` process running e.g. get_references / get_hover gets the leader's real answer with only the leader's single rust-analyzer running.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd`. The `LayeredContext::lsp_request None→Ok(None)` seam is the natural injection point. #diagnostics
\ No newline at end of file

Routing from LayeredContext (code-context) directly to SessionRequestClient (diagnostics) may invert/cycle the dep — the implementer must pick the right layer (tool-op layer in code_context/mod.rs that already has workspace socket/lock access, vs the LayeredContext::lsp_request None→Ok(None) seam) without creating a cycle or a second client. Driving implement → review → commit.'

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,6 +6,15 @@
   id: 01kvfssemkby5w09xzz5bner2n
   text: 'Picked up by /finish (owner: "finish all these"), after ^0j12skf → done @84b134e21. Last task in the follow-up set. Goal: followers route the FULL live-LSP code-context op set through the leader''s session over the existing SessionRequestClient/lsp_request multiplexer, instead of degrading to tree-sitter/empty (today only diagnose_via_leader routes). WATCH the crate dependency direction: LayeredContext lives in swissarmyhammer-code-context; SessionRequestClient/dispatch live in swissarmyhammer-diagnostics; request_ipc in swissarmyhammer-leader-election. Routing from LayeredContext (code-context) directly to SessionRequestClient (diagnostics) may invert/cycle the dep — the implementer must pick the right layer (tool-op layer in code_context/mod.rs that already has workspace socket/lock access, vs the LayeredContext::lsp_request None→Ok(None) seam) without creating a cycle or a second client. Driving implement → review → commit.'
   timestamp: 2026-06-19T11:20:51.091534+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvfszaq0a588k26p3whf77gd
+  text: |-
+    Picked up. Dep-graph (cargo tree + Cargo.tomls): diagnostics -> code-context -> leader-election; code-context -> leader-election (NOT diagnostics); tools -> {code-context, diagnostics, leader-election}. So routing from code-context's LayeredContext into diagnostics' SessionRequestClient would CYCLE (diagnostics already depends on code-context). Routing at the TOOL-OP layer (code_context/mod.rs) is cycle-free because tools already depends on diagnostics — this is exactly how diagnose_via_leader is wired (diagnostics/mod.rs). ALSO LayeredContext methods are sync while SessionRequestClient is async/tokio; option B (inject a routing closure into LayeredContext) would force a block_on inside a sync closure. => CHOSE LAYER A.
+
+    Document-sync gap found: the leader's METHOD_LSP_REQUEST does a RAW session.request with no didOpen/didChange, but the local op path goes through LayeredContext::lsp_request_with_document which syncs the doc first. For a follower op on an arbitrary file the leader may not have it open/fresh. Fix: extend the EXISTING lsp_request params (request_api.rs) with an optional `file_path`; when present the leader sync_opens the doc before session.request. Reuses METHOD_LSP_REQUEST — no new method/transport/envelope.
+
+    Per-op fallback decision: single-request ops route to the leader's session and parse with the SAME code-context parsers — get definition / type_definition / hover / references / implementations / search workspace_symbol. Multi-step ops (get inbound_calls = prepareCallHierarchy+incomingCalls; get code_actions; get rename_edits = prepareRename+rename) hold the client lock across multiple requests via lsp_multi_request_with_document and CANNOT be reproduced by a single lsp_request round-trip; on a follower they fall back to their documented index/tree-sitter best-effort (rename returns can_rename:false). get diagnostics is already covered by the diagnose leader route. Implementing with /tdd next.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-19T11:24:03.680919+00:00
 position_column: doing
 position_ordinal: '8180'
 project: diagnostics

     Per-op fallback decision: single-request ops route to the leader's session and parse with the SAME code-context parsers — get definition / type_definition / hover / references / implementations / search workspace_symbol. Implementing with /tdd next.
   timestamp: 2026-06-19T11:24:03.680919+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvftzsvj4nxk5ctpx480anyc
+  text: |-
+    Implementation landed (TDD, all green). Layer chosen = A (tool-op layer), cycle-free per dep graph; refined with a dependency-inversion seam so reuse is maximal:
+
+    1. crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/layered_context.rs — added `LiveLspRouter` type alias + `with_live_lsp_router` ctor + `live_lsp_router` field. `has_live_lsp()` is true when a router is present; `lsp_request` and `lsp_request_with_document` route through the router when session is None. code-context owns the closure TYPE only; the impl is supplied by the tools layer => code-context does NOT depend on diagnostics (no cycle). Exported LiveLspRouter from lib.rs.
+
+    2. crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/src/request_api.rs — METHOD_LSP_REQUEST extended with an optional `file_path`; leader sync_opens the doc before session.request (mirrors local lsp_request_with_document; fixes the document-not-open gap). Added SessionRequestClient::lsp_request_with_document + lsp_request_envelope. Reuses the EXISTING method/transport/envelope — no second client/socket.
+
+    3. crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/leader_route.rs (NEW) — the ONE shared follower-fallback helper. build_follower_router connects SessionRequestClient from workspace.socket_path()/lock_path(), bridges sync->async via block_in_place + Handle::block_on. Split follower_route_for_op (async, BEFORE the DB handle) + build_layered_context (sync) so the !Send rusqlite Connection is never held across .await (the diagnose-path constraint). ipc_err_to_code_context maps IpcError->CodeContextError typed (NotLeader keeps the leader PID); connect-failure -> None -> index/tree-sitter fallback (documented best-effort for a follower read op).
+
+    4. crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/mod.rs — 6 single-request ops made async + wired through the one helper: get definition, get type_definition, get hover, get references, get implementations, search workspace_symbol.
+
+    Ops routing to the leader + fallback: definition/type_definition/hover/references/implementations route document-scoped (leader syncs the doc); workspace_symbol routes workspace-wide (no file_path). Multi-step ops (get inbound_calls, get code_actions, get rename_edits) hold the client lock across multiple LSP requests and CANNOT be a single round-trip -> they keep their documented index/tree-sitter fallback on a follower (rename returns can_rename:false). get diagnostics is already covered by the diagnose leader route.
+
+    Tests: unit (model-free) layered_context router seam (routes + propagates typed error), request_api leader doc-sync + envelope roundtrip, leader_route ipc-error-mapping + typed-not-leader-on-connect-failure. Gated integration: NEW follower_request_with_document_gets_real_definition_without_leader_preopen in leader_follower_request_ipc.rs — follower issues textDocument/definition via lsp_request_with_document, leader does NOT pre-open, polls until rust-analyzer warm, asserts a REAL definition (helper() on line 0 of main.rs). One leader rust-analyzer, no PPID=1 orphan after.
+
+    really-done verification: diagnostics lib 65 + IPC 3 (incl gated) + diagnose_rust_analyzer 3 + watcher 2 all pass; code-context lib 631; tools code_context 101; clippy (3 crates --all-targets -D warnings) exit 0; cargo fmt --check exit 0; no PPID=1 rust-analyzer orphan. Adversarial double-check running.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-19T11:41:47.762366+00:00
 position_column: doing
 position_ordinal: '8180'
 project: diagnostics

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -34,6 +34,21 @@

     really-done verification: diagnostics lib 65 + IPC 3 (incl gated) + diagnose_rust_analyzer 3 + watcher 2 all pass; code-context lib 631; tools code_context 101; clippy (3 crates --all-targets -D warnings) exit 0; cargo fmt --check exit 0; no PPID=1 rust-analyzer orphan. Adversarial double-check running.
   timestamp: 2026-06-19T11:41:47.762366+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvfve0h2fhymw7w9mx7r6qzp
+  text: |-
+    Double-check returned REVISE — all findings resolved (TDD):
+
+    Finding 1 (HIGH, real bug it caught): the leader returns the FULL JSON-RPC envelope (LspJsonRpcClient::send_request -> read_matching_response returns {jsonrpc,id,result}); the local seam unwraps it via unwrap_lsp_result, but my router path returned it verbatim, so every routed op fed an enveloped value into parsers (parse_definition_locations / parse_workspace_symbols) that expect the bare result -> silent wrong-empty on a follower (defeated the whole task). FIX: route_via_router now applies unwrap_lsp_result (same contract as the session seam) — bare result on success, JSON-RPC `error` envelope -> CodeContextError. Pinned by 2 new RED-first unit tests in layered_context (unwraps result envelope; surfaces error envelope).
+
+    Finding 2 (MEDIUM, verification gap that masked #1): the gated integration test asserted contains("main.rs") on serde_json::to_string of the RAW IPC envelope — which passed on the un-unwrapped value. FIX: the test now drives the REAL get_definition op through a LiveLspRouter backed by the follower SessionRequestClient (production wiring) and asserts SourceLayer::LiveLsp + a real location on line 0 of main.rs. This fails on an enveloped value, so it would have caught #1.
+
+    Finding 3 (LOW): block_in_place panics on a current-thread runtime. FIX: build_follower_router now guards with current_runtime_supports_block_in_place() and degrades to None (index/tree-sitter) on a current-thread runtime instead of handing back a router that panics. 2 unit tests (current_thread -> false, multi_thread -> true).
+
+    Scope note on multi-step ops (acceptance criterion 1 lists inbound-calls/code-actions/rename among "route to the leader"): these hold the client lock across multiple LSP requests (prepareCallHierarchy+incomingCalls / prepareRename+rename) and cannot be reproduced by a single lsp_request round-trip over the current multiplexer; on a follower they keep their documented index/tree-sitter fallback (no panic, no wrong-empty). Routing them would require a NEW multi-request IPC method (a locked multi-step exchange on the leader's session) — genuinely separate work. Proceeding with this partial-but-explicit coverage per the task's "decide per op and be explicit" guidance; filing a follow-up task for the multi-step ops.

+## Review Findings (2026-06-19 06:51)

+Verdict: clean — 0 blockers, 1 warning, 6 nits. The single-request follower routing is correct, cycle-free, and the load-bearing envelope-unwrap fix + strengthened gated test verify out by direct inspection. The 3 multi-step ops are a sound, explicit scope deferral tracked in follow-up ^bp1scnj (project diagnostics, tagged diagnostics, depends_on this task). Engine sweep was partial (2/30 review subtasks failed) but all five load-bearing judgments were independently verified by direct inspection + a clean `cargo build` of the three crates (a cycle would not compile). Warning/nits below are non-blocking polish — not gating the column move.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/mod.rs` — Operation names are hardcoded as string literals in multiple places (dispatch match and notice-conditional match). Membership in a known set should be expressed as data. Consider a module-level `const STATUS_OPERATIONS: &[&str] = &["get status", "rebuild index", "clear status", "lsp status", "detect projects"];` used via `contains` in both match arms.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/layered_context.rs` — `LiveLspRouter` type alias and `with_live_lsp_router` ctor doc comments. (NOTE: on re-inspection both DO carry doc comments — the engine flagged stale; treat as refuted/no-op.)
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/tests/leader_follower_request_ipc.rs` — hardcoded 60s startup timeout could be a named constant `RUST_ANALYZER_STARTUP_TIMEOUT_SECS`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/tests/leader_follower_request_ipc.rs` — hardcoded 60s health-check interval could be a named constant `RUST_ANALYZER_HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECS`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/leader_route.rs` — test leader PID 4321 could be a named constant.
